esql.standard: Every ESQL module begins with the correct BROKER SCHEMA matching the folder path: <TYPE>.<SUBSYS>.<APPNM>.<FUNCNM>.FIL

Compute module structure follows the template: CREATE COMPUTE MODULE Adapter_Compute with a Main() RETURNS BOOLEAN, plus CopyMessageHeaders() and CopyEntireMessage() procedures where the template provides them.
Prefer reference/tree-copy over per-field assignment; avoid unnecessary tree copies. Do not copy the entire message when only headers are needed.
Keep the SUB trigger logic intact: online variant uses isLastMsgGroup; batch variant uses Adapter_IsTrigger. Do not swap or remove them.
Do not introduce hard-coded environment values in ESQL — those belong in the DEV/ACC/PRO .prop files (see rule 4).

artifacts.keep: .project build natures and buildCommands from the reference project unchanged.
application.descriptor as provided by the template.

enforcement.check: BROKER SCHEMA line matches the tokenised folder path in every ESQL file.
No environment-specific literals (paths, queues, endpoints) appear in ESQL.
